full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: net-snmp security update

An update for net-snmp is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4 Advanced Mission Critical Update Support and Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4 Extended Update Support Long-Life Add-On.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for net-snmp is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4 Advanced Mission Critical Update Support and Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4 Extended Update Support Long-Life Add-On.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
net-snmp-5.8-20.el8_4.3.src.rpm
net-snmp-5.8-20.el8_4.3.x86_64.rpm
net-snmp-agent-libs-5.8-20.el8_4.3.i686.rpm
net-snmp-agent-libs-5.8-20.el8_4.3.x86_64.rpm
net-snmp-agent-libs-debuginfo-5.8-20.el8_4.3.i686.rpm
net-snmp-agent-libs-debuginfo-5.8-20.el8_4.3.x86_64.rpm
net-snmp-debuginfo-5.8-20.el8_4.3.i686.rpm
net-snmp-debuginfo-5.8-20.el8_4.3.x86_64.rpm
net-snmp-debugsource-5.8-20.el8_4.3.i686.rpm
net-snmp-debugsource-5.8-20.el8_4.3.x86_64.rpm
net-snmp-devel-5.8-20.el8_4.3.i686.rpm
net-snmp-devel-5.8-20.el8_4.3.x86_64.rpm
net-snmp-libs-5.8-20.el8_4.3.i686.rpm
net-snmp-libs-5.8-20.el8_4.3.x86_64.rpm
net-snmp-libs-debuginfo-5.8-20.el8_4.3.i686.rpm
net-snmp-libs-debuginfo-5.8-20.el8_4.3.x86_64.rpm
net-snmp-perl-5.8-20.el8_4.3.x86_64.rpm
net-snmp-perl-debuginfo-5.8-20.el8_4.3.i686.rpm
net-snmp-perl-debuginfo-5.8-20.el8_4.3.x86_64.rpm
net-snmp-utils-5.8-20.el8_4.3.x86_64.rpm
net-snmp-utils-debuginfo-5.8-20.el8_4.3.i686.rpm
net-snmp-utils-debuginfo-5.8-20.el8_4.3.x86_64.rpm

CVE-2025-68615: A flaw was found in net-snmp. A remote attacker can trigger a buffer overflow in the snmptrapd daemon by sending a specially crafted SNMP packet, causing the daemon to crash and resulting in a denial of service.
